techStack: Message Broker (inferred from 'event-driven-architecture', OutboxProcessor, MessageReplicator, Worker.cs)
Relational Database (inferred from OutboxProcessor and general microservice data storage needs)

services.name: AccountService
services.purpose: Provides API endpoints for managing user accounts and likely handles account-related business logic and events.

services.name: InventoryService
services.purpose: Provides API endpoints for managing product inventory, updating stock levels, and handling inventory-related events.

services.name: OrchestratorService
services.purpose: Coordinates complex business workflows, such as order fulfillment, by initiating and reacting to events from other services. It acts as a central orchestrator for multi-service operations.

services.name: OutboxProcessor
services.purpose: Monitors service outbox tables to reliably publish domain events to the message broker, ensuring atomicity between local database transactions and message publication.

services.name: ClaimCheckProcessor
services.purpose: Processes 'claim check' messages by retrieving large payloads that were stored externally (e.g., in blob storage) using a reference contained in the message, then re-publishing the full message or processing it directly.

services.name: MessageReplicator
services.purpose: Replicates messages between different topics or message brokers, potentially for fan-out scenarios, integration with other systems, or disaster recovery/auditing purposes.

services.name: TestPublisher
services.purpose: A utility application used for manually publishing test events into the system, primarily for development, debugging, and integration testing.

services.name: OrderMaker
services.purpose: Provides shared models and potentially business logic related to creating and managing orders, likely consumed by other services like OrchestratorService or TestPublisher.

services.name: Shared
services.purpose: Contains common definitions, data transfer objects (DTOs), interfaces, or utilities shared across multiple services to promote consistency and reduce code duplication.
